content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 2, 2005

John B. Walker
Classified Personnel Director
P.O. Box 878
Ojai, CA 93024

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-015

This letter is in response to your request for written advice on Government Code § 1090
and its application to the Board of Trustees of the Ojai Unified School District. Under the
provisions of regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), copy enclosed, we must decline your request for advice.

Government Code section 1090 is a conflict of interest law that is applied independent of
(and in addition to) the Political Reform Act's (the "Act") conflict of interest provisions
(sections 87100 et seq.). However, section 1090 is outside the jurisdiction of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. The Commission advises only on laws in the Act.

With respect to the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions we enclose an information
booklet, "Can I Vote? Conflict of Interest Overview" for your information.

Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel, Legal Division

JWW:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\06-015

1 Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked for written advice on Government Code Section 1090 and its application to the Board of Trustees of the Ojai Unified School District.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because Government Code Section 1090 is outside the Commission's jurisdiction; the FPPC only advises on matters within the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter without formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. Document references regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) as basis for declining. Enclosures mentioned: regulation text and informational booklet on conflict of interest.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide advice because Government Code Section 1090 is outside the Commission's jurisdiction; the FPPC only advises on matters within the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines a request for advice regarding Government Code Section 1090's application to a school district board, explaining that Section 1090 falls outside FPPC jurisdiction as the Commission only advises on the Political Reform Act.
